title: Use Bambus2 Scaffolding Module
author: Afiahayati
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.d7n9md
materials: Identify genomic repeats and output them.

steps.title: To install Bambus2 programs, please see their web site
steps.instruction: To install Bambus2 programs, please see their web site: http://www.cbcb.umd.edu/software/bambus/

steps.title: Execute velveth , velvetg as in...
steps.instruction: Execute velveth , velvetg as in the " Getting started " protocol. Please check that " out-dir /Sequences", " out-dir /Roadmaps", and " out-dir /Graph2" files are created.

steps.title: Execute meta-velvetg with -amos_file and -scaffolding options
steps.instruction: Execute meta-velvetg with -amos_file and -scaffolding options. Please check that " out-dir /meta-velvetg.asm.afg" file is created. The file is meta-velvetg contiging result in an AMOS format. ~$ meta-velvetg out-dir -ins_length 260 \ -exp_covs 214_122_70_43_25_13.5 \ -amos_file yes -scaffolding no

steps.title: Create amos bank
steps.instruction: Create amos bank. ~$ bank-transact -m out-dir/meta-velvetg.asm.afg -b bank-dir -cf

steps.title: Use the paired-end information to construct...
steps.instruction: Use the paired-end information to construct a collection of contig links.

steps.title: Bundle the contig links into a collection of contig edges
steps.instruction: Bundle the contig links into a collection of contig edges.

steps.title: Order and orient contigs according to repeat and link information
steps.instruction: Order and orient contigs according to repeat and link information.

steps.title: Output contig FastA file
steps.instruction: Output contig FastA file. ~$ bank2fasta -d -b bank-dir \ | tee contig-file

steps.title: Then, scaffold-file is the final scaffolding...
steps.instruction: Then, scaffold-file is the final scaffolding results in the FastA format.
